mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that immediately depress equity risk premiums and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals robust consumer demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production indicates immediate supply-side contraction that forces earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes yield positive 5d returns in >60% of observations during tightening cycles, or short-horizon Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real rates turn negative, prompting equity investors to rotate into growth assets and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative real rates fail to generate positive 20d returns in >65% of periods, or the inflation-minus-rates spread shows Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and consumption capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60d returns in >60% of cases, or long-horizon Rank IC drops below 0.02 across rolling 3-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDP growth falls below 1.0%, corporate profit margins contract due to weak aggregate demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Sub-1% GDP growth coincides with positive 20d returns in >65% of instances, or threshold signal Rank IC < 0.03 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling retail sales indicates inventory buildup that forces immediate price cuts and earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Inventory buildup signals fail to produce negative 5d returns in >60% of cases, or interaction Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.
